---
title: "Data Loading & Distribution: Historical Basketball Scores"
author: "Pushpendu Biswas"
date: today
format:
  html:
    theme:
      light: flatly
      dark: darkly
    self-contained: true
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

## Setup & Data Loading

This document loads historical basketball game records using **R** and the **tidyverse** library.

```{r load-data}
library(tidyverse)
library(plotly)

# Determine the exact filepath on disk (handling case-sensitivity)
file_path <- if (file.exists("Games.csv")) {
  "Games.csv"
} else if (file.exists("games.csv")) {
  "games.csv"
} else {
  stop("Could not find Games.csv or games.csv in the directory.")
}

# Read the data
games <- read_csv(file_path, show_col_types = FALSE)

# Display a preview of the loaded dataset
glimpse(games)
```

## Win Location Infographic

Below is an infographic showing the distribution of team wins by the type of arena (Home Arena, Away Arena, or Neutral Arena).

```{r win-analysis}
# Categorize the win location
classified_games <- games %>%
  mutate(
    # A game is neutral if it's an All-Star game, OR if we have arena city data and it doesn't match home or away city
    is_neutral = (gameType == 'All-Star Game') | 
                 (!is.na(arenaCity) & !is.na(hometeamCity) & !is.na(awayteamCity) & 
                  arenaCity != hometeamCity & arenaCity != awayteamCity),
    
    win_location = case_when(
      is_neutral ~ 'Neutral Arena',
      winner == hometeamId ~ 'Home Arena',
      winner == awayteamId ~ 'Away Arena',
      TRUE ~ 'Unknown'
    )
  )

# Calculate win counts
counts <- classified_games %>% 
  count(win_location)

home_wins <- counts %>% filter(win_location == "Home Arena") %>% pull(n)
away_wins <- counts %>% filter(win_location == "Away Arena") %>% pull(n)
neutral_wins <- counts %>% filter(win_location == "Neutral Arena") %>% pull(n)
```

```{r render-cards-html}
#| results: asis
# Calculate percentages
total_wins <- home_wins + away_wins + neutral_wins
home_pct <- (home_wins / total_wins) * 100
away_pct <- (away_wins / total_wins) * 100
neutral_pct <- (neutral_wins / total_wins) * 100

# Render a modern, dashboard-style card layout in HTML
html_cards <- sprintf('
<div style="display: flex; gap: 20px; flex-wrap: wrap; justify-content: space-between; margin: 30px 0; font-family: system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif;">
<div style="flex: 1; min-width: 220px; background: linear-gradient(135deg, #1e293b, #334155); color: white; padding: 25px; border-radius: 12px; box-shadow: 0 4px 15px rgba(0,0,0,0.15); text-align: center; border-top: 5px solid #3498db;">
<h3 style="margin-top: 0; font-size: 1.1rem; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 1px; color: #94a3b8;">Home Arena Wins</h3>
<div style="font-size: 3rem; font-weight: 800; margin: 15px 0; color: #38bdf8;">%s <span style="font-size: 1.5rem; font-weight: 400; color: #94a3b8;">(%s)</span></div>
<p style="margin: 0; font-size: 0.95rem; color: #cbd5e1;">Home teams winning on their own court</p>
</div>
<div style="flex: 1; min-width: 220px; background: linear-gradient(135deg, #1e293b, #334155); color: white; padding: 25px; border-radius: 12px; box-shadow: 0 4px 15px rgba(0,0,0,0.15); text-align: center; border-top: 5px solid #2ecc71;">
<h3 style="margin-top: 0; font-size: 1.1rem; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 1px; color: #94a3b8;">Away Arena Wins</h3>
<div style="font-size: 3rem; font-weight: 800; margin: 15px 0; color: #4ade80;">%s <span style="font-size: 1.5rem; font-weight: 400; color: #94a3b8;">(%s)</span></div>
<p style="margin: 0; font-size: 0.95rem; color: #cbd5e1;">Visiting teams winning on the road</p>
</div>
<div style="flex: 1; min-width: 220px; background: linear-gradient(135deg, #1e293b, #334155); color: white; padding: 25px; border-radius: 12px; box-shadow: 0 4px 15px rgba(0,0,0,0.15); text-align: center; border-top: 5px solid #f1c40f;">
<h3 style="margin-top: 0; font-size: 1.1rem; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 1px; color: #94a3b8;">Neutral Arena Wins</h3>
<div style="font-size: 3rem; font-weight: 800; margin: 15px 0; color: #facc15;">%s <span style="font-size: 1.5rem; font-weight: 400; color: #94a3b8;">(%s)</span></div>
<p style="margin: 0; font-size: 0.95rem; color: #cbd5e1;">Wins in neutral cities or All-Star settings</p>
</div>
</div>
', 
format(home_wins, big.mark=","), sprintf("%.1f%%", home_pct),
format(away_wins, big.mark=","), sprintf("%.1f%%", away_pct),
format(neutral_wins, big.mark=","), sprintf("%.1f%%", neutral_pct))

cat(html_cards)
```

```{r render-donut-chart, fig.align='center', fig.width=6, fig.height=4}
# Prepare data for donut chart
df_chart <- data.frame(
  category = c("Home Arena", "Away Arena", "Neutral Arena"),
  count = c(home_wins, away_wins, neutral_wins)
) %>%
  mutate(
    percentage = count / sum(count) * 100,
    label = paste0(category, "\n", format(count, big.mark = ","), " (", round(percentage, 1), "%)")
  )

# Draw an interactive donut chart with hover information
plot_ly(
  df_chart,
  labels = ~category,
  values = ~count,
  type = "pie",
  hole = 0.55,
  textinfo = "label+percent",
  hoverinfo = "text",
  text = ~paste0(category, ": ", format(count, big.mark = ","), " (", round(percentage, 1), "% )"),
  marker = list(colors = c("#3498db", "#2ecc71", "#f1c40f"), line = list(color = "#ffffff", width = 2))
) %>%
  layout(
    showlegend = TRUE,
    legend = list(orientation = "h", x = 0.5, xanchor = "center", y = -0.1),
    margin = list(l = 10, r = 10, b = 10, t = 10)
  )
```
